notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for japan. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: Japan Short-Term Tourism Visa and eVISA

chunks.title: 中国护照日本短期旅游签证建议
chunks.content: 中国护照持有人赴日本短期旅游时，VIZA AI 应推荐 Japan short-term stay tourism visa / eVISA 路线，并根据申请人居住地判断是否可通过 JAPAN eVISA、指定旅行社/代理或日本驻外使领馆办理。不要把日本短期旅游误判成韩国 C-3/K-ETA、加拿大 TRV 或申根签。

chunks.title: Japan eVISA availability and China agency route
chunks.content: MOFA states that JAPAN eVISA is available for short-term tourism for eligible residents. For nationals of China residing in China, applications should be made through an accredited agency; electronic visas with a period of stay of 15 or 30 days may be issued. eVISA travellers must display the Visa Issuance Notice online at the airport; PDF, screenshots and printed copies are not accepted.

chunks.title: Japan short-term stay scope and processing
chunks.content: MOFA describes short-term stay as up to 90 days for tourism, business, visiting friends/relatives and similar activities that do not include remunerative work. Visa applications cannot be made inside Japan. MOFA notes that processing takes approximately one week if all necessary requirements are met, but additional documents or consultation can make it longer.

documents.title: Japan Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Japan application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use Japan eVISA or the competent Japanese mission/agency route where applicable; Japan also provides official visa application forms for paper routes. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/index.html ; https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/visaonline.html. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Japan fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: Japan short-stay intake should collect names, nationality, date/place of birth, sex, marital status, passport details, occupation, address/contact details, purpose of visit, intended arrival and stay length, port of entry, airline/ship, hotel or host, guarantor/inviter if any, travel history, and declarations about criminal/deportation/overstay history.

chunks.title: Japan supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Tourism evidence can include passport, visa application form or eVISA data, photo, itinerary, flight/transport plan, hotel booking, funds/employment evidence, and agency or residence-specific checklist documents. Workflow guardrails: For Chinese residents, VIZA should check the current eVISA/agency route and jurisdiction before promising direct online submission.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.
